scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a brand SKU through readiness, execution, and completion only when production, lot, inventory, and closure evidence aligned.
Missing genealogy and release prerequisites kept the work blocked and produced a bounded exception note instead of an unsupported readiness claim.
Engineering and formulation authority were rejected cleanly and routed without widening the lane.
Conflicting co-manufacturer, ERP, and warehouse evidence was treated as a traceability blocker and prevented unsupported advancement.
Quality-hold pressure moved the work into hold posture and preserved final release authority with QA.
Completion was refused until reconciliation, inventory, and signoff evidence existed, keeping state transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested tenant release rules, supplier repositories, and system-of-record context instead of guessing.
Conflicting dated FDA timing guidance and tenant interpretation were summarized and escalated rather than collapsed into a false compliance answer.
Supplier substitution pressure was escalated to supplier-quality, procurement, and QA owners without unsupported approval.
Tenant terminology was adapted without losing FDA, CPSC, and GS1 baseline controls or execution-state discipline.
A post-run nonconformance moved the case into rework posture and escalated formal disposition instead of allowing unsupported closure.

notes: All 11 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to CPG brand execution, co-manufacturer coordination, lot and inventory discipline, and release-safe completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the audit contract.
